title: lycopodium annotinum subsp. alpestre
intro: Lycopodium annotinum subsp. alpestre, also known as alpine club moss, is a subspecies of club moss that belongs to the plant family Lycopodiaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its expectorant properties, which help to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The medicinal properties of alpine club moss include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, contributing to its potential health benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved respiratory function, and enhanced immune system. Alpine club moss is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, and can also be found in various herbal combinations and formulations to support respiratory health.
